title: glycosmis pentaphylla var. macrocarpa
intro: Glycosmis pentaphylla var. macrocarpa, also known as the large-fruited Glycosmis, is a variant of the Glycosmis pentaphylla plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of Glycosmis pentaphylla var. macrocarpa are typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being applied topically to wounds and skin conditions.
